data.title: Dell COO states no major impact from tariffs, company's second-quarter earnings call mentions deflationary input costs. Dell stock weak.
data.description: Dell Technologies Inc. (NYSE: DELL) is addressing trade and tariff uncertainties confidently, with COO Jeff Clarke assuring that the company anticipates minimal impact moving forward and has no intentions to raise prices for customers. Despite this, DELL stock is displaying notable weakness currently. During the second-quarter earnings call, Clarke stated that the company's full-year guidance incorporates all known tariff implications and they maintain a positive outlook on pricing. Dell witnessed deflationary input costs in the second quarter, further supporting their position. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: UTStarcom announces unaudited financial results for the first half of 2025, revealing business performance until June 30.
data.description: UTStarcom, a leading global telecommunications infrastructure provider, based in Hangzhou, China, recently released its unaudited financial report and business update for the first half of 2025. The report covers the period ending June 30, 2025. The company, listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol UTSI, shared the important financial figures for this period, showcasing its performance in the industry.

data.title: Youlife Group Inc. forms joint venture with Beijing Galbot Co., Ltd. to advance vocational education in China. #YOUL #partnership
data.description: On August 29, 2025 in Beijing, Youlife Group Inc. (Nasdaq: YOUL), a prominent blue-collar lifetime service provider in China, revealed the signing of a joint venture deal between its subsidiary and Beijing Galbot Co., Ltd. This strategic agreement is aimed at fostering collaboration and enhancing business operations within the two entities.

data.title: HBAR aiming for $0.33 in September, may rally to $1.25+ by 2026, analysts predict.
data.description: Analysts predict a bullish trend for HBAR with a target of $0.33 next month despite current consolidation. They further forecast targets exceeding $1.25 by May 2026, indicating significant growth potential in the coming years.

data.title: EU aluminum industry, facing a 50% tariff, is under pressure. Brussels racing to protect 40 billion euros industry and 250,000 jobs. Scrap metal shipments to US exempt, causing European recyclers to suffer. Plants shutting down, risking survival.Character count: 261
data.description: President Donald Trump's fluctuating tariff policy poses a challenge for the aluminum supply chain, highlighting its vulnerabilities. With the EU enduring a 50% tariff for almost three months, urgent actions are being taken to counteract the adverse impacts. Brussels is crafting emergency strategies to safeguard its €40 billion aluminum industry, which directly employs 250,000 individuals and supports a further 1 million jobs. The existing policy asymmetry, where finished aluminum exports to the U.S. confront a 50% tariff while scrap metal shipments are spared, has led to a surplus of scrap heading to the U.S. Consequently, European recyclers are deprived of essential raw materials. The repercussions are dire, with plants cutting down operations not just to maintain profits but to sustain their operations. According to Paul Voss, European Aluminium's head, firms have only weeks to address the looming crisis. Industry experts caution that the U.S. aluminum tariffs not only jeopardize the supply chain but also food security. More comprehensive details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Only 20% of organizations are prepared for Automation and AI, according to GBTEC's research. Process excellence is crucial.
data.description: In Bochum, Germany, a recent report by GBTEC highlights that despite significant investments in AI and automation by organizations, many lack the operational maturity required for success. The report, titled The 2025 Process Excellence and AI-Readiness Report, underscores the importance of process excellence in facilitating effective business transformation through automation and AI. Based on input from 600 senior business and operations leaders in ANZ, Europe, and North America, the report sheds light on the critical role of process optimization in ensuring successful integration of technologies.
